mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 3.8%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and supports consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 3.8%, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the CPI inflation rate signals healthy demand expansion without price overheating, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The spread between quarterly GDPC1 growth and monthly CPI inflation shows no positive correlation with 60-day SPX returns, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and strong business investment, which positively impacts S&P 500 returns specifically during early-cycle economic recoveries.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in over 50% of observations during early-cycle periods, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts exceeding 10% over three months signals weakening consumer credit conditions and confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances despite the HOUST decline, or the forward return differential between triggered and non-triggered periods is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with stable or falling federal funds rates indicates a Goldilocks environment where economic expansion is supported without aggressive monetary tightening, boosting equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and non-rising FEDFUNDS does not produce a positive Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly higher than the market average.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and food services revenue reflects sustained consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS momentum shows no positive correlation with 60-day SPX forward returns, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 2.5%, the market anticipates aggressive Fed tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving negative forward returns specifically in high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY, or the interaction term between inflation and rate expectations shows no statistical significance in a regression model.
